description:
Offered: every semester
This core course serves as an introduction to the breadth and nature of human services work. Students will become acquainted with a variety of human service agencies at local, state and federal levels (i.e., their organization and administration, potential as hiring placements, and scope of services). The course facilitates students’ awareness of the theoretical perspectives, issues and ideological dilemmas in the field of human services.
Note: This course is a prerequisite for most other Human Services courses.
Meets General Education Requirement(s): CV
